sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of low back pain that started approximately 2 hours after raking leaves in his yard.
He described the pain as tightening up and associated with pins and needles in his right foot.
He has had similar episodes in the past, with the most recent episode occurring 3 years ago, which resolved on its own.
He reports that the pain is worse with standing and sitting, but improved with a hot shower.
He denies taking any medication for the pain prior to presentation, but reports using ice and 2 Advil, which provided no relief.
His partner reports similar pain when the patient stands up.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in his right leg.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibited pain on flexion and palpation of the l5 area.
His dorsiflexion was normal, and pulses were equal in all extremities.
The patient's partner reports pain when the patient stands up.
Test Results: The patient had a normal x-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a probable lumbar strain with possible radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar strain; suspected radiculopathy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5mg orally twice daily for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve flexibility and strength.
I instructed the patient to rest and avoid heavy lifting and bending.
I advised the patient to follow up with me in 2 weeks if the pain does not improve, at which point we will consider further testing, including an MRI.
Follow-up: The patient is to follow up with me in 2 weeks for re-evaluation.
